frontier: What is the minimum number of edges q(6) in a 6-uniform pairwise-intersecting hypergraph with cover number exactly 6? Current bracket: [14, 18].
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Implemented the 5-cover verifier correctly and validated on PG(2,5) as a regression test. Built q6_verifier.py — a no-argument Python entrypoint that (a) checks pairwise-intersection O(m²·6) and (b) rejects witnesses admitting a 5-cover via exact C(N,5) hitting-set enumeration. Registered three kill fixtures: star (tau=1, 5-cover), two-disjoint (fails intersection), and PG(2,5) (31 edges, V=31, known tau=6).
verified:
Verifier domain: A 6-uniform hypergraph (list of edges, each a frozenset of exactly 6 distinct integers >= 0).
Verifier predicate: pairwise-intersecting AND no subset of 5 vertices hits every edge.
Artifact: q6_verifier.py, SHA-256: 23b46923866daa9c5b6f7660dc6c6c3b1b4a3dc1561f3962e703e7abb5efa33c
Domain kill fixtures: 1/1 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 1/1 rejected correctly
ALL KILL FIXTURES PASSED
PG(2,5) regression: 31 edges, 31 vertices, pairwise-intersecting=true, no_5_cover=true, tau_ge_6=true, accepted=True. The prior normalization bug (modular inverse of line coefficients computed as iterative multiplier instead of Fermat inverse) is eliminated — this implementation builds PG(2,5) via the explicit affine-line construction with correct point numbering, avoiding modular arithmetic entirely.
result: The exact-witness verifier pipeline is now correct and replayable. PG(2,5) confirms the verifier accepts a known tau=6, 31-edge hypergraph and rejects the two canonical kill fixtures with correct branch reasons. The literature bracket q(6) ∈ [14, 18] remains unchanged — no new witness was found. The productive route remains: isomorph-free orderly generation with tau-deficiency pruning, targeting 14 ≤ m ≤ 17 with V ≤ 29 (per Barát Lemma 6.4).
next_gate: Implement an isomorph-free orderly generation engine for 6-uniform intersecting families with tau-deficiency pruning (per McKay's canonical augmentation or Barát's method). Generate candidates with 14 ≤ m ≤ 17; for each candidate run the verified q6_verifier.py. A single valid witness with m < 18 improves the bound; m=14 settles q(6)=14.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training.
